Click any supplier — in the chart legend or the ledger below — to inspect its cost drift, lead time and concentration, and send its verdict to Bridge.

Spend · 90d$1.01M

At the current slope, CM1 on Screen Protector breaches target in ~6 weeks — silently, because the base price never moved. This is the cost invoice-watching misses: freight and duty creep, not the number on the PO.

Operations AI · Suppliers
Live3 signalsSynced just now
Price increase
Shenzhen Wei landed cost up 9%
It's <b>freight, not base</b> — the unit price never moved. <b>$37K</b> added to 90-day COGS. At this pace, CM1 on Screen Protector breaches target in ~6 weeks.
Lead-time change
Chiang Mai stretched 24 → 30 days
Manufacturing lead time grew six days. Reorder points on Canvas Tote and Water Bottle need to move <b>earlier</b>, or the category gaps before goods land.
Concentration
65% of COGS from 2 suppliers
Accessories are <b>sole-sourced</b> from Vietnam Leather — one delay stops the whole category. A second source breaks the single point of failure.

Lead-time capital drag — deposit frozen during manufacturing
Deposit cash locked before goods can be sold
$313Kfrozen across 4 suppliers
deposit paid → manufacturing → delivery → first sellable day
Capital, not just pricea cheaper unit with a longer freeze can cost more

freeze

Bangkok Home Goods Co.local
$0 · net-30 terms
3d
freeze

Shenzhen freezes the most cash — $165K locked for 24 days — while Chiang Mai freezes it longestat 30. Bangkok’s net-30 terms freeze nothing: a structural advantage worth leaning on when two suppliers can ship the same thing.

MConsole note
Read
5 suppliers carrying $1.01M of 90-day COGS. 6 items at risk across 2 suppliers. Your top 2 sources hold 65% of all spend.
Exposure
Shenzhen's landed cost is drifting up on freight (+38%) — the base price hasn't moved. At the current slope, CM1 on Screen Protector drops below target in ~6 weeks, silently. And $165K of deposit cash sits frozen 24 days before those goods can be sold.
Directive
Open a freight renegotiation or alternate-forwarder quote with Shenzhen now — the drift is logistics, not product, so it is negotiable. Add a second accessories source to break the Vietnam single-point dependency. Hold the local and OEM suppliers; they are stable.
Ask Magellan anything…
